findings.description: Header and footer layout structure lost
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the header has names on the left and the state seal centered, while the footer has side-by-side address blocks separated by a logo. In the HTML, these elements are collapsed and stacked vertically.
The HTML rendering contains a styled version of the page content, followed by an unformatted plain-text dump of the same content at the bottom, forcing the reader to encounter the information twice.
The HTML rendering contains a styled version of the page content, followed by an unformatted plain-text dump of the same content at the bottom.

findings.description: List structure lost in duplicated text
findings.evidence: In the unformatted plain-text section at the bottom of the HTML, list items 'a' through 'j' under section B.1 lose their line breaks and are rendered as a single run-on paragraph.

findings.description: Broken heading hierarchy. The document contains a single <h3> tag without any preceding <h1> or <h2> tags.
findings.evidence:
The HTML contains '

Wehner, Judy

' as the first and only heading tag in the document, skipping h1 and h2 levels entirely. Other logical headings (e.g., 'ATTACHMENT 1') are implemented as bolded paragraphs instead of semantic heading tags.
